title: Lastrea angulariloba
uses: Lastrea angulariloba, also known as the angular-lobed lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In some cultures, it is used as a diuretic to help with urinary issues. However, its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific research and validation.

constituents: Lastrea angulariloba contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. preparations: Lastrea angulariloba, also known as the angular cloak fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Lastrea angulariloba, also known as the angular lady fern, is a species of fern within the Lastrea genus. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea angulariloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Lastrea angulariloba has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
